evals.name: external-rich-context
evals.prompt: Generate a meeting prep note for today's 2pm meeting with Acme. Assume the Google Calendar, Gmail, Notion, and web-search MCP steps have already been run and their results are in the attached fixture file. You don't need to call any MCPs — use the fixture as your input. Output the prep note as a markdown file called prep-note.md in the outputs folder, matching the Notion page structure the skill prescribes.
evals.expected_output: A one-page markdown prep note following the skill's template — BLUF At-a-glance section first, attendees with per-person context, recent context bullets, likely agenda, exactly three talking points, optional watch-outs. Should surface the pilot-slip context and Alex's stated preference for firm dates.

evals.name: mixed-internal-external
evals.prompt: Generate a meeting prep note for the 10am Q2 partnership review. Treat the attached fixture as the output of Calendar/Gmail/Notion/web-search tool calls. Output prep-note.md in the outputs folder using the skill's Notion-page structure. The user's company domain is nutripy.com — distinguish internal from external attendees accordingly.
evals.expected_output: Prep note where external attendees (Marco, Sofia) get fuller treatment than internal attendees (Tom, Lisa). Should flag Sofia as new-to-us and Marco's request for better unit economics. Three talking points.

evals.name: cold-meeting-no-email-history
evals.prompt: Generate a meeting prep note for the 4:30pm intro call with Jamie Okafor from Finch Capital. Use the attached fixture as the output of the tool calls. Output prep-note.md. There's no email history — handle that gracefully.
evals.expected_output: Prep note that acknowledges the zero email history (doesn't fabricate one), uses the web research to build Jamie's bio, infers a plausible agenda from the intro framing, and suggests talking points appropriate for a first VC intro.
